nodes.label: AI as coding assistant for ideas and explanations
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unhelpful for complex issues
nodes.grounding_span: I use it when I need ideas, explanations, syntactical questions, or to get examples to help with coding

nodes.label: AI as validation tool after own research
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to rely on searching the web, and then conversing with AI to validate my understanding

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs delegator
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate with AI rather than delegate a complete task

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: it kept giving me a 'fix' that is the exact same code as what I already had

nodes.label: Workplace-sanctioned AI use and its side effects
nodes.label_negative: Unsupervised AI reliance leading to quality issues
nodes.grounding_span: I see developers getting lazy and solely rely on Copilot

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: Copilot is provided by my company... to help speed up the development process

nodes.label: Epistemic understanding and validation
nodes.grounding_span: conversing with AI to validate my understanding

nodes.label: Professional competence and code quality
nodes.grounding_span: I have to be more careful while reviewing since I noticed code is overcomplicated, not performant

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for coding assistance
nodes.grounding_span: Copilot wasn't 100% perfect but it was close. I ended up using most of the code

nodes.label: Frustration with AI unhelpful loops
nodes.grounding_span: After numerous attempts of telling Copilot that no code changes happened, it still kept giving me the same code snippet

nodes.label: Concern about over-reliance on AI by colleagues
nodes.grounding_span: I see developers getting lazy and solely rely on Copilot. This affects me greatly

nodes.label: Hope for AI to become highly reliable like Stack Overflow
nodes.grounding_span: I hope to see AI evolve into the next stack overflow where 90% of the time it is correct

nodes.label: Iterative cross-validation — uses web search and AI to complement each other
nodes.grounding_span: I would go read articles online... Then, I would go back and ask AI

nodes.label: Collaborative dialogue with AI — provides context and problem statement, then iterates
nodes.grounding_span: Typically it starts off with a background or some context of what I am trying to achieve. Then, I give the problem statement
